entries.problem: Create tensors and move to device
entries.trigger: Model input preparation, weight initialization, or converting NumPy arrays for GPU
entries.snippet:
x = torch.tensor(data, dtype=torch.float32).to(device)
x = torch.from_numpy(arr).float()      # shares memory with numpy array
device = torch.device('cuda' if torch.cuda.is_available() else 'cpu')
entries.minimal_notes: from_numpy shares memory — modifying one modifies the other. Call .clone() to break the link.
entries.common_bug: Tensor on CPU passed to a model on GPU raises RuntimeError — always call .to(device) on both model and inputs.

entries.trigger: Custom training — any time you are not using HuggingFace Trainer
entries.snippet:
model.train()
for batch_X, batch_y in dataloader:
    batch_X, batch_y = batch_X.to(device), batch_y.to(device)
    optimizer.zero_grad()       # must come before forward
    out = model(batch_X)
    loss = criterion(out, batch_y)
    loss.backward()
    optimizer.step()
entries.minimal_notes: zero_grad() must be called before backward() — omitting it accumulates gradients across batches silently.
entries.common_bug: Calling loss.item() inside the training loop keeps the computation graph alive — only call .item() for logging, never for intermediate ops.

entries.problem: Run inference without building a computation graph
entries.trigger: Validation loop, serving, embedding generation — anywhere you do not need gradients
entries.snippet:
model.eval()
with torch.no_grad():
    preds = model(X.to(device))
    probs = torch.softmax(preds, dim=-1)
    labels = probs.argmax(dim=-1).cpu().numpy()
entries.minimal_notes: model.eval() disables dropout and switches BatchNorm to use running stats. torch.no_grad() disables gradient tracking. Both are required.
entries.common_bug: Forgetting model.eval() during validation — dropout randomly zeroes activations, producing different results on every forward pass.

entries.problem: Save and load a model checkpoint
entries.trigger: Saving the best model during training or resuming from a checkpoint
entries.snippet:
# Save
torch.save({'epoch': epoch, 'model_state': model.state_dict(),
            'optim_state': optimizer.state_dict(), 'loss': loss}, 'ckpt.pt')

# Load
ckpt = torch.load('ckpt.pt', map_location=device)
model.load_state_dict(ckpt['model_state'])
optimizer.load_state_dict(ckpt['optim_state'])
entries.minimal_notes: Always save optimizer state alongside model state — without it, adaptive optimizers (Adam, AdamW) reset their momentum on resume.
entries.common_bug: torch.load without map_location always loads to the device it was saved on — pass map_location=device to avoid CUDA errors when loading on CPU.

entries.trigger: Flattening before a Linear layer, adding/removing dims for conv or attention
entries.snippet:
flat  = x.view(batch_size, -1)           # (B, C, H, W) -> (B, C*H*W)
expd  = x.unsqueeze(1)                   # (B, L) -> (B, 1, L)
swap  = x.permute(0, 2, 1)              # (B, L, D) -> (B, D, L)
entries.minimal_notes: view requires contiguous memory — call .contiguous() before view if permute was applied, or use reshape which handles non-contiguous tensors.
entries.common_bug: x.view(-1, 512) after permute raises RuntimeError about non-contiguous tensors — use x.contiguous().view(-1, 512) or x.reshape(-1, 512).
